Caption: the homogeneous free energy at different vacancy concentration θ v . With increasing vacancy concentration ( θ v increases), the energy minimum near the Li metal phase ( ξ = 1 ) is increased and the minimum disappeared when θ v / θ v 0 surpasses 100, indicating that there is only one local minimum at ξ = 0 and the Li metal will transform into void phase spontaneously in this case.

The stability diagram for Li-ASSBs based on the simulation results using the phase-field model. The yellow area is the range for detachment of Li/SE interface (the void growth will not be impeded when the battery is operated under the conditions within this area). The red area is the range for short circuit caused by mechanical Li penetration, which is described as progressive mechanical short circuit. The green area, which is the overlaps of the difference set of the yellow and red area within whole parameter space, is the anticipated area for stable operation.
